releaseNotes: v0.34.0 — PHI scrubbing + multi-page PDF + customer-readiness sweep. (1) The Liberty Stock importer now SCRUBS patient-shaped columns transparently instead of hard-rejecting the file. Real pharmacy systems often include patient columns by default and operators frequently can't change the export config; v0.33 forced them to do something they couldn't do. v0.34 strips those columns at the parse boundary and tells the operator what was ignored ("2 columns matching 'patient', 1 matching 'mrn'"). PHI values briefly exist in Dart memory during decode but are never copied into any LibertyStockRow, sync op, server post, log, or disk write. The PHI fragment blocklist expanded from 13 to 29 patterns covering insurance, claims, encounters, and benefit identifiers. (2) The PDF parser processes pages independently. v0.33 flattened words across all pages, which collapsed because Syncfusion returns per-page Y coordinates; multi-page Liberty reports (any non-trivial pharmacy) silently lost most rows. v0.34 establishes column geometry per page and supports up to 3000 rows across many pages. (3) Customer-readiness test sweep: NDC edge cases (Excel-stripped leading zeros, OCR'd letters, controlled substances), direct xlsx_reader unit tests, performance smoke tests with tightened ceilings (3000-row xlsx parses in 94ms), multi-device reconciliation race regression. Three-reviewer audit: Sonnet and Codex independently converged on the multi-page geometry concern AND the column-name leak concern. All findings fixed in the same session. 712 tests passing (+33 vs v0.33.0). Auto-updater rolls v0.33.0 → v0.34.0 on next launch.
